data.title: Ares Management launches European long-term investment fund vehicle
data.description:
Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) announced on Thursday the launch of its first European long-term investment fund vehicle, the Ares European Strategic Income ELTIF Fund.

The alternative investment manager said the semi-liquid perpetual direct lending fund will provide individual investors in the European Economic Area an expanded access to the company's European Direct Lending strategy.

data.title: Musk's X eyes investment, trading platform for users - report
data.description:
• Users of the Elon Musk-owned social media app X could be able to do more than just make posts: They could execute trades.
  • X will "soon" have the capability to use the app to "to transact your whole financial life on the platform," CEO Linda Yaccarino told the Financial Times, adding that will include making investments or trades, as well as sending money to someone else.
  • She added that X is also weighing the launch of a credit or debit card, which could debut later this year.
  • X has already made a foray into offering financial products as it announced in January it will offer the digital wallet X Money, also later this year. It is partnered with Visa (V) on that.
  • "A whole commerce ecosystem and a financial ecosystem is going to emerge on the platform that does not exist today," Yaccarino noted.

data.title: Bank of Montreal to acquire Toronto-based wealth manager Burgundy for ~$625M
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) (TSX:BMO:CA) announced on Thursday an acquisition of the Toronto-based wealth manager Burgundy Asset Management for ~$625M.

The purchase price is payable in BMO common shares and includes a $125M holdback to be paid subject to Burgundy maintaining certain assets under management 18 months post-closing.

The deal may also see an earn-out component in the future based on the achievement of certain growth targets.

Burgundy had ~$27B in AUM as of May 31.

The acquisition is expected to expand BMO Wealth Management and strengthen the company's offering in the Canadian Investment Counsel space.

Burgundy is set to operate as part of BMO Wealth Management and CEO Robert Sankey will continue to lead the business.

data.title: Trump extends TikTok ban deadline by 90 days
data.description:
U.S. President Donal Trump announced on Thursday he has signed an executive order, extending the deadline for 90 days to bring U.S. assets of TikTok under American ownership from China's ByteDance (BDNCE). 

"I've just signed the Executive Order extending the Deadline for the TikTok closing for 90 days (September 17, 2025). Thank you for your attention to this matter!" President Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Trump has extended the deadline for the third time on enforcing the congressionally mandated TikTok ban, which was originally set to take effect in January.

Earlier in May, Trump first indicated that he would consider pushing back the original June 19 deadline. The president seeks to find a buyer – or buyers – for the social media platform, creating a new U.S.-based entity that would steer TikTok away from Chinese purview.

data.title: Terreno Realty acquires industrial property in New York for ~$16M
data.description:
Industrial REIT Terreno Realty (NYSE:TRNO) announced on Thursday the acquisition of an industrial property in Long Island City Queens, New York, for ~$16M.

The property consists of one industrial distribution building containing about 36,000 sq. ft. on 1.0 acres, and is 100% leased through May 2028 to an insulation distributor.

data.title: Iron Mountain announces commencement of work on the U.S. Treasury department contract
data.description:
Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) announced on Thursday the commencement of work on a recently awarded contract by the U.S. Department of the Treasury.

The data center operator and provider of document storage services said it is actively digitizing documents and leveraging its proprietary intelligent digitization solution as part of the engagement.

data.title: Delta Air Lines raises dividend by 25% to $0.1875
data.description:
• Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) declares $0.1875/share quarterly dividend, 25% increase from prior dividend of $0.1500.
  • Forward yield 1.58%
  • Payable Aug. 21; for shareholders of record July 31; ex-div July 31.
  • See DAL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Skye confirms phase 2 data on nimacimab for obesity expected later this year
data.description:
Phase 2a data on Skye Bioscience's (NASDAQ:SKYE) cannabinoid-1 receptor blocker (CB1) for obesity, nimacimab, should be released in late Q3 or early Q4, the company's CEO confirmed to Seeking Alpha.

In its Q1 earnings call in May, Punit Dhillon said that top-line weight loss data from a 26-week primary analysis of the CBeyond study would be out then. 

Speaking to Seeking Alpha on the sidelines of the BIO International Convention in Boston, Dhillon explained how nimacimab could be an alternative or even complementary to GLP-1s currently dominating the weight loss space from pharma giants Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY). Nimacimab is also under investigation in combination with Novo's semaglutide, the active ingredient in Wegovy for weight loss and Ozempic for type 2 diabetes.

Nimacimab is not the first CB1 inhibitor for obesity. Monlunabant, a small molecule developed by Inversago Pharma and eventually acquired by Novo, showed modest weight loss, but also came with reports of mild to moderate neuropsychiatric events. Rimonabant, another oral CB1 inhibitor, was developed by Sanofi (SNY) and approved in Europe, but was eventually withdrawn due to a high number of neuropsychiatric side effects. 

Dhillon said that data so far on nimacimab, a large molecule, monoclonal antibody, avoids the neuropsychiatric events that have plagued oral CB1s in the past because the asset doesn't target receptors in the brain.

Instead, nimacimab is focused on blocking peripheral CB1 signaling, aiming to block that. Organs with a high number of CB1 ligands -- activators -- such as the kidneys, liver, and pancreas, as well as adipose tissue and the digestive system can lead to inflammation and metabolic dysfunction if CB1 ligands are activated.

One of the drawbacks of GLP-1s, according to Dhillon, is the high discontinuation rates, mainly because of gastrointestinal side effects. He contended that nearly two-thirds of individuals on these drugs stop using them within a year. "If you can have weight loss with minimal side effects, we believe that can be super beneficial."

Another advantage nimacimab could provide, said Dhillon, is that it is only targeting fat, meaning it can help preserve lean muscle mass and minimize muscle wasting. 

Although a monoclonal antibody, Dhillon noted that nimacimab is administered through subcutaneous injection.

As far as capital, he added that the company has enough cash for a "one-year plus cushion," though for phase 3, "we believe a larger partner could help us ramp up faster."

data.title: Bank of England holds interest rates, warns economic and geopolitical unpredictability
data.description:
The Bank of England left interest rates unchanged Thursday as the U.K. economy struggles with heightened geopolitical risks, persistent inflation, potential trade disruptions from proposed US tariffs, and mixed domestic economic data.

The widely anticipated decision keeps the central bank’s benchmark interest rate at 4.5%.

In a statement, the central bank said that its Monetary Policy Committee voted in favor of leaving rates unchanged with an 6-3 majority. Three MPC member voted for a 25-basis-point reduction to 4.0%.

The headline inflation in the UK ticked lower to 3.4% in May; however, it remains significantly above the BoE’s 2% target, and surged over the past few days as Israel-Iran conflict has led to higher oil prices 

Still, core inflation came in slightly below expectations at 3.5%, and services inflation fell sharply to 4.7%, in line with forecasts.

The median Market Participants Survey ((MaPS)) respondent is expecting 50 basis points of bank rate cuts this year.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Does X mark the spot for Elon Musk to consolidate his companies?
data.description:
Elon Musk has a history of contemplating the consolidation of his various companies. As early as 2012, Musk publicly mused about the idea of creating a parent holding company to facilitate the movement of capital and resources between his businesses, such as Tesla and SpaceX. 

"They are separate corporations, so capital cannot be moved between them. Am starting to consider whether it would make sense to create a parent corporation that would own the stock, which would make that easier. Not sure if that is feasible or sensible, but am thinking about it."

At various times since, Musk has often justified consolidation as a way to maximize synergies, streamline operations, and accelerate innovation.

More recently, Musk merged his artificial intelligence company xAI with his social media platform X (formerly Twitter). He described the decision as a way to combine “data, models, compute, distribution, and talent” to unlock synergies between AI development and a massive distribution platform. Musk stated:

"xAI and X's futures are intertwined. Today, we officially take the step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution, and talent," he advised on X.

During an interview on CNBC in late May, Elon Musk said it's not out of the question to merge his AI startup with Tesla if shareholders approve.

Elon Musk's companies include Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), SpaceX (SPACE), Neuralink, The Boring Company, and xAI.

In an interesting development, U.S. Steel (X) is expected to stop trading on the New York Stock Exchange once its acquisition by Nippon Steel is finalized. That means the symbol X will be up for grabs. Musk has had an attachment to the "X" branding persisted throughout his career, including his first venture with the X.com online banking and financial services platform.
